message:
refactor: make VM user configurable (default: mooby)

- Add VMConfig.User to config with default "mooby"
- Update Dockerfile to use ARG VM_USER, persist as ENV
- Update build.sh to accept VM_USER env var
- Update CLI commands (attach, cp, logs) to use cfg.VM.User
- Update stockyard-init.sh to use VM_USER env var
- Update vm-image README with configuration docs

The VM user was previously hardcoded as "vscode". Now it can be
configured at build time via VM_USER env var and at runtime via
the config file's vm.user setting.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -13,6 +13,9 @@ LABEL description="Development VM image for Claude Code sessions with Firecracke
 # Prevent interactive prompts during package installation
 ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
 
+# Configurable VM user (default: mooby)
+ARG VM_USER=mooby
+
 # ============================================================================
 # Section 1: Base System
 # ============================================================================
@@ -106,16 +109,16 @@ RUN npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code
 # Section 5: User Setup
 # ============================================================================
 
-# Create vscode user with sudo privileges
-RUN useradd -m -s /bin/bash -G sudo vscode \
-    && echo "vscode ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWD:ALL" >> /etc/sudoers.d/vscode \
-    && chmod 0440 /etc/sudoers.d/vscode
+# Create VM user with sudo privileges
+RUN useradd -m -s /bin/bash -G sudo ${VM_USER} \
+    && echo "${VM_USER} ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWD:ALL" >> /etc/sudoers.d/${VM_USER} \
+    && chmod 0440 /etc/sudoers.d/${VM_USER}
 
-# Set up Rust for vscode user
-RUN su - vscode -c "curl --proto '=https' --tlsv1.2 -sSf https://sh.rustup.rs | sh -s -- -y"
+# Set up Rust for VM user
+RUN su - ${VM_USER} -c "curl --proto '=https' --tlsv1.2 -sSf https://sh.rustup.rs | sh -s -- -y"
 
 # Create workspace directory
-RUN mkdir -p /workspace && chown vscode:vscode /workspace
+RUN mkdir -p /workspace && chown ${VM_USER}:${VM_USER} /workspace
 
 # ============================================================================
 # Section 6: SSH Configuration
@@ -126,12 +129,12 @@ RUN mkdir -p /var/run/sshd \
     && sed -i 's/#PasswordAuthentication yes/PasswordAuthentication no/' /etc/ssh/sshd_config \
     && sed -i 's/#PubkeyAuthentication yes/PubkeyAuthentication yes/' /etc/ssh/sshd_config
 
-# Add SSH key for vscode user (for debugging)
-RUN mkdir -p /home/vscode/.ssh \
-    && echo "ssh-ed25519 AAAAC3NzaC1lZDI1NTE5AAAAIH3MQD2+f46FzfwZLiouCPkmcQGWmFBkc2d9aA4qjIMZ jesse@paradise-park.local" > /home/vscode/.ssh/authorized_keys \
-    && chmod 700 /home/vscode/.ssh \
-    && chmod 600 /home/vscode/.ssh/authorized_keys \
-    && chown -R vscode:vscode /home/vscode/.ssh
+# Add SSH key for VM user (for debugging)
+RUN mkdir -p /home/${VM_USER}/.ssh \
+    && echo "ssh-ed25519 AAAAC3NzaC1lZDI1NTE5AAAAIH3MQD2+f46FzfwZLiouCPkmcQGWmFBkc2d9aA4qjIMZ jesse@paradise-park.local" > /home/${VM_USER}/.ssh/authorized_keys \
+    && chmod 700 /home/${VM_USER}/.ssh \
+    && chmod 600 /home/${VM_USER}/.ssh/authorized_keys \
+    && chown -R ${VM_USER}:${VM_USER} /home/${VM_USER}/.ssh
 
 # ============================================================================
 # Section 7: Stockyard Integration
@@ -197,12 +200,15 @@ RUN curl -fsSL -o /boot/vmlinuz \
 # Section 9: Finalize
 # ============================================================================
 
+# Persist VM_USER for runtime scripts
+ENV VM_USER=${VM_USER}
+
 # Set environment variables
-ENV GOPATH=/home/vscode/go
-ENV PATH="/home/vscode/.cargo/bin:/usr/local/go/bin:${PATH}"
+ENV GOPATH=/home/${VM_USER}/go
+ENV PATH="/home/${VM_USER}/.cargo/bin:/usr/local/go/bin:${PATH}"
 
 # Default working directory
-WORKDIR /home/vscode
+WORKDIR /home/${VM_USER}
 
 # Expose SSH port
 EXPOSE 22

@@ -25,6 +25,9 @@ Output: `output/rootfs.ext4` (~4GB)
 ### Configuration Options
 
 ```bash
+# Custom VM user (default: mooby)
+VM_USER=myuser ./build.sh
+
 # Custom image name/tag
 IMAGE_NAME=my-vm IMAGE_TAG=v1 ./build.sh
 
@@ -37,7 +40,7 @@ ROOTFS_SIZE=20G sudo ./convert-to-rootfs.sh
 - **Languages**: Python 3, Node.js 20, Go 1.22, Rust
 - **Tools**: git, gh (GitHub CLI), Claude Code
 - **Networking**: Tailscale (userspace mode for Firecracker)
-- **User**: `vscode` with passwordless sudo
+- **User**: `mooby` with passwordless sudo (configurable via `VM_USER`)
 
 ## VM Configuration

@@ -17,9 +17,11 @@ cd "${SCRIPT_DIR}"
 # Configuration with defaults
 IMAGE_NAME="${IMAGE_NAME:-stockyard-vm}"
 IMAGE_TAG="${IMAGE_TAG:-latest}"
+VM_USER="${VM_USER:-mooby}"
 
 echo "=== Building Stockyard VM Image ==="
 echo "Image: ${IMAGE_NAME}:${IMAGE_TAG}"
+echo "VM User: ${VM_USER}"
 echo ""
 
 # Step 1: Build the stockyard-snapshot Go binary
@@ -45,6 +47,7 @@ cd "${SCRIPT_DIR}"
 echo ""
 echo "=== Step 2: Building Docker image ==="
 docker build \
+    --build-arg VM_USER="${VM_USER}" \
     -t "${IMAGE_NAME}:${IMAGE_TAG}" \
     -f Dockerfile \
     .

@@ -5,6 +5,9 @@
 
 set -e
 
+# VM_USER is set in Dockerfile, default to mooby if not set
+VM_USER="${VM_USER:-mooby}"
+
 LOG_FILE="/var/log/stockyard/init.log"
 mkdir -p /var/log/stockyard
 
@@ -72,7 +75,7 @@ done
 
 # Setup workspace permissions
 if [ -d /workspace ]; then
-    chown -R vscode:vscode /workspace 2>/dev/null || true
+    chown -R "${VM_USER}:${VM_USER}" /workspace 2>/dev/null || true
     echo "Workspace permissions set"
 fi
 
@@ -87,9 +90,9 @@ if command -v tailscale &>/dev/null; then
     fi
 fi
 
-# Setup Claude Code hooks for vscode user
+# Setup Claude Code hooks for VM user
 if [ -f /etc/stockyard/claude-hooks.json ]; then
-    su - vscode -c "/usr/local/bin/setup-claude-hooks.sh" 2>/dev/null || true
+    su - "${VM_USER}" -c "/usr/local/bin/setup-claude-hooks.sh" 2>/dev/null || true
 fi
 
 # Create run directory for snapshot socket

@@ -1,7 +1,7 @@
 #!/bin/bash
 # setup-claude-hooks.sh
 # Sets up Claude Code hooks for auto-snapshots
-# This script should be run as the vscode user
+# This script should be run as the VM user (mooby)
 
 set -e
